text.title: vui-privacy-security Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the vui-privacy-security methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Users MUST be informed BEFORE voice capture begins, with a clear, dated privacy notice listing what is recorded, where it is stored, retention, and how to delete.
rule.rationale: Post-hoc notices fail GDPR + CCPA; legal exposure is real.

rule.statement: Recording state MUST be indicated visually (LED / icon) AND audibly (start/end chime). Silent recording is forbidden.

rationale.source: Apple HIG; W29 Article 29 WP opinion on smart devices

rule.rationale: Without indicators, users cannot give informed consent at the moment of capture.

rule.statement: Sensitive actions (payment, account change, health data access) MUST require step-up authentication beyond the initial voice session (PIN, biometric, second-factor).
rule.rationale: Voice can be spoofed or shoulder-surfed; one-factor voice cannot protect sensitive actions.

rule.statement: Transcripts MUST be redacted of PII (names, account numbers, addresses, dates of birth, health terms) BEFORE leaving the device or being logged. Server-side redaction is insufficient as the only mechanism.
rule.rationale: Once PII reaches a 3rd-party log, removal is hard or impossible; on-device redaction is the only reliable mitigation.

rule.statement: Spec MUST declare a retention period for transcripts (≤30 days for non-essential) AND a user-accessible 'delete my voice history' control. Indefinite retention is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Users have legal right to delete; defaulting to indefinite retention violates the principle.

rule.statement: If audience MAY include minors, additional protections (COPPA / GDPR Art. 8) MUST be declared: parental consent gate, no behavioural profiling, enhanced data-minimisation.
rule.rationale: Minor data has heightened legal protections; default consumer flow does not meet them.
